hub_result.content:
I’m loading the dispatcher command file first, then I’ll inspect the repo state and execute the requested workflow with the step overrides you specified.I have the dispatcher workflow and overrides. Next I’m reading the dispatch plan to find the first eligible worker and confirm whether this turn should spawn, retry, notify, or stop.The first eligible row is `N-04`: it is `⚠️ ABANDONED`, its dependency `R-03` is `✅`, and the dispatcher workflow says to retry it before any pending `⬜` row. I’m switching it back to pending through the Meridian tool, then I’ll spawn and run it with `codex` in `pane_bridge` mode.`resume-worker` failed because the lifecycle store has no record for `N-04`, which means the dispatch table and lifecycle state are out of sync. I’m checking the implementation and current plan state once before deciding whether this is recoverable or should be escalated as a manual intervention.The failure is real: `resume-worker` hard-requires an existing lifecycle entry, and `N-04` does not have one. I’m checking whether the plan directory still has a dispatch lifecycle file for this worker; if it doesn’t, this falls into manual intervention because the dispatcher cannot safely reconstruct the abandoned thread state from the plan alone.I found that `run` can create a fresh lifecycle entry even when one is missing, but doing that blindly could duplicate an interrupted worker if there are already partial `N-04` artifacts or commits. I’m checking for any existing `N-04` report or commit history so I can decide whether recovery should be “rerun” or “escalate.”The current state is not safe to advance automatically: the plan says `N-04` is abandoned, but the lifecycle file has no `N-04` entry, and the branch already contains an `N-04` report plus `N-04` commits. I’m notifying through the configured reply channel and pausing here rather than rerunning a worker that may already have landed.Dispatch is paused on `N-04`.

`resume-worker` failed with `Worker not found in lifecycle state: N-04`, and the state is inconsistent:
- [dispatch_plan.md](/Users/yzliu/work/Meridian/docs/branch/feat-cli-external-integration/dispatch_plan.md) marks `N-04` as `⚠️ ABANDONED`.
- [dispatch_threads.json](/Users/yzliu/work/Meridian/docs/branch/feat-cli-external-integration/dispatch_threads.json) has no `N-04` lifecycle entry.
- [N-04_report.md](/Users/yzliu/work/Meridian/docs/branch/feat-cli-external-integration/dev_history/v1_round/N-04_report.md) already exists, and the branch already contains `N-04` commits (`fed7934`, `665409b`).

I sent a notify to the configured `web:feat-cli-external` reply channel asking for manual reconciliation and an explicit `continue` or `skip N-04` response. I did not spawn or rerun any worker after detecting this, and I made no code changes.
